name: Mercury
description: The smallest planet in our solar system and closest to the Sun—is only slightly larger than Earth's Moon. Mercury is the fastest planet, zipping around the Sun every 88 Earth days.
fact: Mercury is shrinking as its iron core slowly cools and solidifies.

name: Venus
description: Spinning slowly in the opposite direction from most planets, Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, with surface temperatures hot enough to melt lead.
fact: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.

name: Earth
description: Our home planet is the only place we know of so far that’s inhabited by living things. It's also the only planet in our solar system with liquid water on the surface.
fact: Earth is the only planet not named after a god or goddess.

name: Mars
description: Mars is a dusty, cold, desert world with a very thin atmosphere. There is strong evidence Mars was—billions of years ago—wetter and warmer, with a thicker atmosphere.
fact: Mars is home to Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the solar system.

name: Jupiter
description: Jupiter is more than twice as massive as the other planets of our solar system combined. The giant planet's Great Red spot is a centuries-old storm bigger than Earth.
fact: Jupiter has the shortest day in the solar system, lasting only 10 hours.

name: Saturn
description: Adorned with a dazzling, complex system of icy rings, Saturn is unique in our solar system. The other giant planets have rings, but none are as spectacular as Saturn's.
fact: Saturn is the only planet that could float in water because of its low density.

name: Uranus
description: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun, and has the third-largest diameter in our solar system. It was the first planet found with the aid of a telescope.
fact: Uranus rotates on its side, nearly 90 degrees from the plane of its orbit.

name: Neptune
description: Dark, cold and whipped by supersonic winds, ice giant Neptune is the eighth and most distant planet in our solar system.
fact: Neptune was the first planet to be predicted by mathematical calculations.
